channel.title: Home on Hi, I am Prasoon!
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Hi, I am Prasoon!

item.title: Autoregulation
item.description:
This is the second post in the series on systems biology. In the previous post, we discussed the dynamics of a simple (single interaction) transcription network. In this post, we will get our hands dirty by looking at the dynamics of a complex transcription network with multiple interactions.

GOAL: Before we dig further, it’s important to clarify the objective of this post. Framing it as the questions we want to answer: How do we find meaningful patterns in a complex web of thousands of genes? And how does a simple loop completely alter the speed limit ($T_{1/2} = \tau$) we just established?

item.title: Introduction to Transcription Networks
item.description:
This is the first post in the series on systems biology. In this post, we will discuss the basics of transcription networks and how they are used to solve what Alon calls the “cognitive problem” of the cell.

DNA, Genome, and Genes - What is the Difference?

These three terms are heard quite frequently in the biological sciences, and most of the time they are used interchangeably. However, they are different concepts and have different meanings but can be put under the same umbrella. The purpose of this section is to develop a sense of what they are and their relationship. We need to think of them not as a chain but as nested levels of organization.

item.title: Hello World!
item.description:
Hello World!

This is a test blog post.

# here is test code
import os
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

LaTeX

Here is an example of inline LaTeX: $E = mc^2$.

Test for display math:

$$ {\sqrt {n}}\left(\left({\frac {1}{n}}\sum _{i=1}^{n}X_{i}\right)-\mu \right)\ {\xrightarrow {d}}\ N\left(0,\sigma ^{2}\right) $$

item.title: CV
item.description:
March 2025 - Present Student Researcher → PhD Fritz Lipmann Institute, Germany
Advisor Dr. H. Melike Dönertas
April 2026 - Present PhD in Computational Biology
  • Research: Theoretical Aging Models and Machine Learning
  • Continuing development of biologically informed frameworks for modeling epigenetic aging and DNA methylation dynamics
March 2025 - April 2026 ML Student Researcher
  • Investigated the stochastic nature of aging and developed dynamical systems models of DNA methylation
  • Leading and developing a mathematically grounded framework for epigenetic aging, modeling the cellular dynamics of DNA methylation
  • Analyzing the mechanistic and biological interpretation of traditional ElasticNet-based epigenetic predictors
  • Designing and maintaining data engineering pipelines for large-scale DNAm datasets, including preprocessing and package management
October 2022 - April 2026 MSc in Physics Friedrich-Schiller-Universität Jena, Germany Advisor Dr. Sebastiano Bernuzzi
  • Thesis: Machine Learning for Gravitational Waves
  • Developing a machine learning-based gravitational waveform model mlgw_bns_HOM incorporating higher-order modes and precession effects to reduce parameter estimation runtime
  • Designed a pipeline enabling training data generation, model training, and testing on standard CPUs, eliminating the need for GPUs or high-performance computing
October 2023 - July 2024 ML Research Assistant Max Planck Institute for Biogeochemistry Advisor Dr. Nuno Carvalhais
  • Contributed to the EarthNet Initiative by leveraging high-resolution satellite imagery to develop data-driven impact early warning systems for droughts and heat waves
  • Conducted research on spatio-temporal land surface forecasting using 3D and (2+1)D CNNs
  • Achieved a 50% reduction in training time compared to best models while maintaining competitive performance metrics
July 2019 - May 2022 BSc in Physics Birla Institute of Technology, Mesra Supervisor Dr. Uma Papnoi
  • Theoretical astrophysics; first peer-reviewed publication on thermodynamics and gravitational lensing of charged black holes in perfect fluid dark matter.

Publication

  • Mark Olenik, Ismail Güderer, Yi Wang, Tayyab Alvi, Prasoon Pandey, and H. Melike Dönertas. “Design and analysis strategies for robust microbiome ageing research”. FEBS Lett. 590 (2026). doi:10.1002/1873-3468.70397

item.title: Research
item.description:
This page highlights two research directions: my current work on Aging and Longevity, and my previous MSc research in Gravitational Waves.

Aging and Longevity

Over the past few months, I have been working with Dr. Mark Olenik and Dr. Melike Dönertaş on a project aimed at understanding the stochastic nature of aging.

Chronological age simply measures the time elapsed since birth. Aging, however, refers to the progressive decline in biological function that increases vulnerability to disease and death. Quantifying this process, often described as estimating an individual’s biological age, is central to understanding age-related diseases and designing interventions that promote healthy longevity. One widely used framework for this purpose is the concept of an aging clock.
